prompt:
Design a Heritage Club & Collections Operations API to manage multi-tenant student clubs, events, curated digital collections, and controlled distribution logistics in an offline-capable environment. The system serves five primary roles: Administrator (tenant-level governance and configuration), Club Manager (club profile, departments, events, and member lifecycle), Counselor/Reviewer (approves group-leader onboarding and sensitive withdrawals), Group Leader (pickup-point operations and performance visibility), and Member (limited access to entitled content and event participation). API capabilities must support tenant-isolated management of club organizations, including club profiles, internal departments/groups, and member join/leave/transfer workflows with reason codes and effective dates; member statuses must include Active, Pending, Suspended, Alumni, and Banned, with every status change captured in an immutable log. Event operations must include registration, check-in capture, attendance reconciliation, and resource download tracking so dashboards can calculate conversion rate (registrations ÷ eligible members), attendance rate (checked-in ÷ registrations), active members (at least one check-in or download in the last 30 days), and check-in time distribution (15-minute buckets). Pickup-point management must maintain US-style addresses, business hours by weekday, capacity limits, and temporary closures; group-leader onboarding requires document metadata capture and a two-step review (Submitted → Approved/Rejected). Commission rules must support fixed-per-order and percentage-of-eligible-amount models with effective date ranges and per-tenant caps.

The backend uses Django REST Framework to expose resource-oriented APIs for authentication, RBAC authorization, tenant configuration, clubs/events/members, pickup points/leaders/settlements, content assets, and analytics/report export, with MySQL as the sole persistence layer for fully offline Docker deployment. Accounts must support local username/password only; passwords require at least 12 characters with complexity rules, store salted hashes, and enforce lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes; sessions expire after 8 hours of inactivity and are revoked on password change. Multi-tenant modeling must support Organization → Warehouse → Zone → Location hierarchies; locations store dimensions (inches), load limit (lbs), temperature zone enum, and restricted-handling flags, with capacity tracked as integer “slots.” Configuration changes must be audited (who/when/what) and versioned with rollback allowed within 30 days. Masterpiece/Stamp asset management must use a unified content model (title, creator, period, style, medium, size, source, copyright status, tags) with draft/published states and monotonically increasing version numbers; bulk import/export must accept CSV and JSON with strict schema validation and duplicate detection by tenant + external_id. Content security must enforce chapter-level ACLs, entitlement via subscription flags or offline redeem codes (12 characters, single-use, 90-day expiry), and configurable download/share permissions; downloads use expiring tokens (10 minutes) plus rate limits (60 requests/minute per user) and watermarking that overlays tenant name, username, and timestamp on exported images/PDFs. Settlements generate monthly on the 1st at 2:00 AM local time, with a 7-day hold; withdrawals are ledger-based (no online payments), capped at $500/day and 2 requests/week, with blacklist support and mandatory reviewer approval for any single request over $250. Observability must include structured audit logs, metrics snapshots, and report exports stored locally, with PII fields (address, phone) encrypted at rest and partially masked in standard query responses.

Backend stack: Django

Database: MySQL
